title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Crispy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic, crispy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) at home with our easy step-by-step recipe. Perfect for a delicious meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Korean seafood pancake
best Korean seafood pancake at home
seo.h1: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A plate of crispy Korean Seafood Pancake (Haemul Pajeon) with dipping sauce, garnished with green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Golden-brown and packed with fresh seafood, this Haemul Pajeon is a delightful Korean classic.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the edges and soft in the middle, generously studded with an assortment of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. It offers a delightful combination of textures and flavors, making it a perfect appetizer or light meal that truly captures the essence of Korean comfort food. This traditional dish is a staple at Korean tables, especially popular as a rainy-day snack or accompaniment to makgeolli.

instructions.title: Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly rinse the mixed seafood and pat dry. If using larger pieces, chop them into bite-sized chunks. Cut the green onions into 3-4 inch long pieces. Thinly slice the yellow onion and mince the garlic. Set all prepared ingredients aside.

instructions.title: Make the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and cold water until smooth. Ensure there are no lumps. Crack in the egg and whisk again until fully incorporated. The batter should be thin but not watery, similar to crepe batter.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients for Pancake
instructions.text: Gently fold the prepared mixed seafood, sliced yellow onion, and minced garlic into the pancake batter. Be careful not to overmix, as this can develop the gluten in the flour and make the pancake tough.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan and Add Batter
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the bottom of the pan evenly. Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it out to form a thin, even layer. Immediately arrange half of the green onion pieces neatly over the top of the batter.

instructions.title: Cook the First Side Until Golden
instructions.text: Let the pancake cook undisturbed for about 5-7 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the batter appears mostly set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake to ensure even crisping. This helps create that desirable crunchy texture.

instructions.title: Flip and Cook the Second Side
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side, pressing down gently with the spatula to ensure even cooking and browning. The pancake should be golden brown and cooked through on both sides. Remove from the pan and place on a cutting board.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: While the first pancake cooks, prepare the dipping sauce. In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds. Add red chili flakes if you prefer a little heat.

instructions.title: Repeat and Serve
instructions.text: Repeat steps 4-6 with the remaining batter and green onions to make the second pancake. Once both pancakes are cooked, slice them into wedge-shaped pieces and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Enjoy while warm and crispy!

tips: Use very cold water for the batter to achieve a crispier texture. You can even add an ice cube to the water.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook one pancake at a time to ensure proper browning and crispiness. Use enough oil for frying to get those delicious crispy edges.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking. It is best enjoyed hot and fresh. Pair it with a cold glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) for an authentic experience.
article:
Haemul Pajeon: Your Guide to the Ultimate Korean Seafood Pancake

Imagine a sizzling, golden-brown pancake, generously studded with vibrant green onions and an array of succulent seafood, served with a tangy, savory dipping sauce. This isn't just any pancake; it's Haemul Pajeon, a beloved staple of Korean cuisine that tantalizes taste buds with its unique blend of textures and flavors. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a curious home cook, mastering this iconic Korean Seafood Pancake is a rewarding culinary journey that brings the bustling streets of Seoul right into your kitchen. From its crispy edges to its tender, flavorful interior, Haemul Pajeon offers a delightful experience that’s perfect as an appetizer, a side dish, or even a light meal.

## What is Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake?

Haemul Pajeon is a savory Korean pancake, a variant of "jeon," which refers to a dish made by pan-frying ingredients coated in a flour batter. The name itself offers a clue to its main components: "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pa" refers to green onions (scallions), which are the defining characteristics of this particular jeon. Unlike the sweet pancakes enjoyed for breakfast in many Western cultures, Haemul Pajeon is a distinctly savory creation, often enjoyed with a side of makgeolli (Korean rice wine) or as an accompaniment to other Korean dishes.

What sets Haemul Pajeon apart is its incredible texture contrast. The exterior is cooked to a beautiful golden crisp, often with delightfully crunchy edges, while the interior remains soft, chewy, and packed with the fresh flavors of green onions and various seafood. Common seafood additions include shrimp, squid, mussels, and oysters, providing a delightful brininess that complements the slightly sweet and pungent green onions. It's a dish that embodies comfort food in Korea, frequently found in street food stalls, traditional restaurants, and bustling markets, celebrated for its satisfying taste and heartwarming appeal. This authentic Korean Seafood Pancake is truly a culinary gem.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need to gather:

For the Pancake Batter:
* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* 1/4 cup glutinous rice flour (optional, for added chewiness)
* 2 tablespoons cornstarch (for extra crispiness)
* 1 large egg
* 1.5 cups very cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
* 1/2 teaspoon salt

For the Seafood & Vegetables:
* 2 cups green onions, cut into 3-4 inch lengths (about 2 bunches)
* 1/2 cup shrimp, peeled, deveined, and chopped into bite-sized pieces
* 1/2 cup squid, cleaned and cut into rings or bite-sized pieces
* 1/4 cup mussels or oysters (optional), cleaned
* 1/4 small onion, thinly sliced
* 1 small red chili pepper (optional, for garnish and slight heat), thinly sliced
* 3-4 tablespoons cooking oil (vegetable, canola, or grapeseed oil) for frying

For the Dipping Sauce:
* 2 tablespoons soy sauce
* 1 tablespoon rice vinegar
* 1 teaspoon sugar
* 1 teaspoon sesame oil
* 1 teaspoon sesame seeds
* 1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)
* 1 clove garlic, minced (optional)

Ensure your seafood is fresh or properly defrosted and patted dry to prevent excess moisture in the batter. The quality of your green onions is also paramount, as they are a star of this delicious Korean Seafood Pancake.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Haemul Pajeon might seem intricate, but with these clear steps, you'll be frying up perfect pancakes in no time.

1.  **Prepare Seafood and Vegetables:** Thoroughly clean all seafood and pat them dry with paper towels. Cut shrimp and squid into bite-sized pieces. Trim the white ends of the green onions and cut them into 3-4 inch segments. Thinly slice the onion and red chili (if using).
2.  **Make the Dipping Sauce:** In a small bowl, whisk together all the dipping sauce ingredients: soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, gochugaru, and minced garlic. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Batter:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, glutinous rice flour, cornstarch, and salt. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg with the very cold water (or broth). Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking until just combined. A few lumps are okay; do not overmix, as this can lead to a tough pancake. The batter should be the consistency of a thin pancake batter.
4.  **Assemble and Cook the Pancake:** Heat 2 tablespoons of cooking oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
    *   Once the pan is hot, ladle about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of batter into the pan, spreading it thinly to form a circular base.
    *   Immediately arrange a generous layer of green onion segments, seafood pieces, sliced onion, and red chili slices evenly over the batter. You want a good distribution of toppings in every bite.
    *   Pour a little more batter over the toppings, just enough to bind them together, but don't completely cover them.
    *   Drizzle another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake. Cook for 4-6 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to curl up.
    *   Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Press down gently with the spatula to ensure even cooking and to help create a crispy crust on the second side.
    *   Cook for another 3-5 minutes, or until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is cooked through.
5.  **Serve:** Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Slice it into wedges or squares. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Repeat the process for any remaining batter and ingredients.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving the perfect Haemul Pajeon involves a few key techniques:

*   **Cold Water is Key:** Using very cold water (or even ice water) for your batter helps inhibit gluten development, resulting in a crispier pancake.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** Overmixing develops gluten, which can make your pancake tough and chewy rather than tender inside and crispy outside. A few lumps are perfectly fine.
*   **Generous Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil! A good amount of oil in the pan is crucial for achieving that irresistible golden-brown, crispy crust. You might even drizzle a bit more around the edges before flipping.
*   **Hot Pan:** Ensure your pan is hot enough before adding the batter. This helps create an immediate sizzle and crispness.
*   **Even Distribution:** Take the time to spread your green onions and seafood evenly across the batter. This ensures every bite is flavorful and well-balanced.
*   **Press Down Gently:** After flipping, gently pressing the pancake with your spatula helps maximize contact with the hot pan, promoting even cooking and a crispier surface.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed hot off the pan. The crispiness fades as it cools, so gather your diners before you start cooking!

## Serving Suggestions

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile. It shines as a standalone appetizer, particularly when paired with a chilled glass of makgeolli or soju, making it a popular choice for a casual gathering. It also makes a fantastic side dish to a larger Korean meal, complementing dishes like bulgogi, galbi, or kimchi jjigae. For a light lunch or dinner, serve it as the main course, perhaps alongside a simple salad or a bowl of steamed rice. The tangy dipping sauce is absolutely essential, cutting through the richness of the pancake and adding another layer of flavor.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment with variations to suit your taste or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** Easily made vegetarian by omitting the seafood and adding an abundance of other vegetables like mushrooms (shiitake or oyster mushrooms work well), bell peppers, zucchini, or even finely chopped kimchi for Kimchi Pajeon.
*   **Different Seafood:** Don't limit yourself to shrimp and squid. Scallops, small clams, or even imitation crab meat can be excellent additions. A mixed seafood bag from your local Asian market is often a convenient option.
*   **Spice It Up:** For those who love a kick, add a teaspoon of gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the batter or increase the amount of gochugaru in the dipping sauce.
*   **Flour Mix:** While the recommended flour mix provides the best texture, you can use just all-purpose flour if glutinous rice flour or cornstarch isn't available, though the texture might be slightly less chewy or crispy.
*   **Cheesy Pajeon:** For a modern twist, sprinkle some mozzarella cheese on top during the last few minutes of cooking for a gooey, savory treat. This versatile Korean Seafood Pancake truly offers something for everyone.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Its delightful combination of crispy exterior, tender interior, and the fresh burst of seafood and green onions makes it an irresistible treat. With this comprehensive guide, you're now equipped to recreate this authentic Korean Seafood Pancake in your own kitchen, bringing a taste of Korea to your table. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to savor every delicious bite of this iconic Korean delicacy!
